With Next.js 16.1.1:
```

Module not found: Can't resolve './src/lib/tailwind'
  1 | import type { Config } from "tailwindcss";
> 2 | import { someCu

source.title: Empty chat widget when opening an archived worktree agent session
source.body:
Opening a recently-archived, worktree-isolated Copilot agent session shows an **empty chat widget** with no transcript.

### Cause
Archiving a worktree-isolated session deletes its on-disk worktree (keeping the branch). On resume, the Copilot SDK requires the session's working directory to exist to bring the session up — the only path to reading the transcript. Both `resumeSession` and its `createSession` fallback pass the deleted worktree path and fail with `Directory does not exist or cannot be accessed`, so the session is restored with `0 turns` → empty model. The failure was also swallowed

dup_of.title: Agents window: corrupted session file silently loads as empty instead of surfacing error
dup_of.body:
## Bug Description

When opening an old session in the Agents window, if the Copilot SDK rejects `session.resume` due to a validation error in the session file, the agent host silently falls back to `createSession` with the same ID. This results in the session appearing to load successfully but showing **0 turns** — all conversation history is lost without any user-facing error.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Have an existing agent session with conversation history
2. The session file becomes corrupted (in this case, a compaction metadata field fails validation)
3. Open the session in the Agents w

merge_note: Closing as a duplicate of #319244, which covers the same "silently loads as empty instead of surfacing an error" bug. Tracking the fix there. (Written by Copilot)

source.title: torch.ldexp backward returns zero gradient w.r.t. mantissa for negative integer exponents
source.body:
### 🐛 Describe the bug

`torch.ldexp(input, other)` computes `input * 2**other`, so the gradient w.r.t. `input` is `2**other`. When `other` is an integer tensor with a negative value, the backward pass returns **0** instead, while the forward pass is correct:

```python
import torch
m = torch.tensor([0.5], requires_grad=True)
e = torch.tensor([-1], dtype=torch.int32)
torch.ldexp(m, e).backward()
print(m.grad)   # tensor([0.])  — should be 0.5

# float exponent is correct:
m = torch.tensor([0.5], requires_grad=True)
torch.ldexp(m, torch.tensor([-1.0])).backward()
print(m.grad)   # tensor([0.500

dup_of.title: torch.ldexp returns incorrect gradient w.r.t. mantissa for negative exponents
dup_of.body:
## Bug

`torch.ldexp(x, exponent)` appears to return an incorrect gradient with respect to `x` when `exponent` contains negative values.

Mathematically:

```python
torch.ldexp(x, e) == x * 2**e
```

So the derivative with respect to `x` should be:

```python
2**e
```

However, for `e = -1`, PyTorch returns a gradient of `0.0` instead of `0.5`.

## Minimal Reproducer

```python
import torch

m = torch.zeros(4, dtype=torch.float32)
e = torch.tensor([-1, -1, -1, -1], dtype=torch.int32)
cot = torch.ones(4, dtype=torch.float32)

result = torch.func.grad(
    lambda x: (torch.ldexp(x, e) * cot).sum

merge_note: Closing as a duplicate of #186556, which reported this same `torch.ldexp` negative-exponent gradient bug on 2026-06-07. Apologies for the duplicate — my search used narrower terms and missed it. #186556 is the canonical report.

source.title: [vllm] [2.12 regression][Inductor] assert_size_stride "expected size 8192==512" in AOT-compiled graph during Nomic bert_with_rope pooling warmup
source.body:
## Summary

On the vLLM torch-2.12 upgrade branch, the vLLM CI job **"Language Models Test (Extended Pooling)"** fails deterministically on `test_nomic_max_model_len.py::test_use_rope_scaling_legal[model_info1]` (Nomic `bert_with_rope` pooling model). During `torch.compile` warmup the AOT-compiled graph trips a stride assertion:

```
torch._inductor ... assert_size_stride(arg9_1, (512, 64), (64, 1))
AssertionError: expected size 8192==512, stride 64==64 at dim=0
This error most often comes from a incorrect fake (aka meta) kernel for a custom op.
RuntimeError: Engine core initialization failed.

dup_of.body:

On `torch==2.12.0` + `triton==3.7.0`, the Extended-Pooling pytest job for the `nomic-ai/nomic-embed-text-v2-moe` model fails inside an Inductor AOT-compiled forward with a runtime stride/shape assertion:

```
File "/root/.cache/vllm/torch_compile_cache/torch_aot_compile/<hash>/inductor_cache/ra/cra7eslwqvpfcdh65siik6ag5goi4wdpxi6ivjnoyjbr4zci2oje.py", line 664
    assert_size_stride(arg9_1, (512, 64), (64, 1))
AssertionError: expected size 8192==512, stride 64==64 at dim=0
This error most often comes from a incorrect fake (aka meta) kernel for a custom op.
```

The compiled functio

merge_note: Duplicate of #184549, which already tracks this exact failure. Bisect details (vLLM #41277) posted there. Closing.

dup_of.title: Show CI failure details to non-collaborator PR authors
dup_of.body:
### What is the problem this feature will solve?

CI is now only visible to collaborators. External contributors can see that CI failed, but not why, so they have to ask a collaborator to learn about the CI status. 

### What is the feature you are proposing to solve the problem?

If CI fails on a PR from a non-collaborator, have `nodejs-github-bot` post a short comment with the failure summary, failing test names etc.

*[Original idea](https://openjs-foundation.slack.com/archives/C019Y2T6STH/p1782994917772279?thread_ts=1782991063.018469&cid=C019Y2T6STH) by @RafaelGSS*

This is also similar in

source.title: Temporal: Month names are missing
source.body:
### Version

26.4.0

### Platform

Fedora 44, with Node.js installed using moonrepo proto.

```text
Linux danielpc 7.0.9-205.fc44.x86_64 #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Thu May 21 16:31:48 UTC 2026 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

_No response_

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

Call `toLocaleString` on a `Temporal.PlainYearMonth` using one of the string `month` options (`long` or `short`):

```js
new Temporal.PlainYearMonth(2026, 6).toLocaleString('en-US', {calendar: 'iso8601', month: 'long'})
new Temporal.PlainYearMonth(2026, 6).toLocaleString('en-US', {calendar: 'iso8601', month: 'short',

dup_of.title: Missing month in `Intl.DateTimeFormat` output when using `calendar: 'iso8601'` in Node v24.13.0 and later
dup_of.body:
### Version

v24.15.0, v25.9.0

### Platform

```text
Darwin Jasons-MacBook-Pro-2.local 25.4.0 Darwin Kernel Version 25.4.0: Thu Mar 19 19:30:44 PDT 2026; root:xnu-12377.101.15~1/RELEASE_ARM64_T6000 arm64
```

### Subsystem

_No response_

### What steps will reproduce the bug?

Run the following code using Node.js **24.13.0 or later**:

```js
const dtfWithIsoCalendar = new Intl.DateTimeFormat('en-US', {
    dateStyle: 'full',
    timeStyle: 'long',
    timeZone: 'UTC',
    calendar: 'iso8601'
});

const date = new Date('2024-09-09T08:00:00Z');

console.log(dtfWithIsoCalendar.format(date));
``

merge_note:
Technically a duplicate of #63041.

There is standards discussion ongoing regarding what the behaviour should be here, as the DateTimeFormat operation is being run with the PlainYearMonth default calendar `iso8601`, and it's unclear what that means from a DateTimeFormat perspective.

source.title: plugin: concrete assertion from non-empty interface can fail with equivalent itabs
source.body:
### Go version

go version go1.24.6 linux/amd64

### Output of `go env` in your module/workspace:

```shell
GOARCH='amd64'
  GOOS='linux'

I built and ran a program using two plugins. The first plugin creates a non-empty interface value through an empty-interface to non-empty-interface
  assertion. The second plugin receives that value and performs a concrete type assertion.

  ```go
  // go.mod
  module example.com/itabrepro

  go 1.24
  ```

dup_of.title: encoding/json: JSONv2 baseline changes SyntaxError and UnmarshalTypeError offsets
dup_of.body:
Hi 👋🏻 

While testing [k6](https://github.com/grafana/k6) against Go tip, we noticed failures that appear to be caused by changed `encoding/json` error offsets after JSONv2 became the baseline implementation.

We are not sure whether this offset change is intended behavior with JSONv2, or whether this is an unexpected compatibility issue in the v1 `encoding/json` API.

The first Go commit where we observe the behavior is: https://github.com/golang/go/commit/e62d3e6e897175a07aa44a7b2c7f99700072f22f

That commit says:

> By JSONv2 becoming baseline, the v1 "encoding/json" will be implemented und

merge_note:
Hi, thanks for the report.

There are 3 reported differences.

> Difference 1: the element type name in the error.

This is due to v1 `json.RawMessage` now being a type alias to `jsontext.Value`. The Go stdlib has historically moved types before and not considered it a breaking change. For example, `os.FileInfo` has been moved to `fs.FileInfo`. This means that the string name can change, but code properly checking the exact type will correctly work (even if they still reference the old type decl

source.title: tests/ui/codegen/huge-stacks.rs fails in a constrained QEMU environment where the guest has 1 GB of RAM
source.body:
The failure is:
```
thread 'main' panicked at tests/ui/codegen/huge-stacks.rs:20:10:
called `Result::unwrap()` on an `Err` value:
Os { code: 11, kind: WouldBlock, message: "Resource temporarily unavailable" }
```

The failure occurs while creating the test thread:
```
std::thread::Builder::new()
    .stack_size(5 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024)
    .spawn(func)
    .unwrap();
```

spawn() returns Err(EAGAIN) because the kernel refuses to create a thread with the requested 5 GB stack under the available resources.

**Environment**
Rust: 1.98.0-nightly
Linux
QEMU guest
Guest RAM: 1024 MB

**Reproduction**
